Video.summary: TFH celebrates Alfred Hitchcock and the masterful storytelling that still keeps audiences on the edge of their seats.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Looking back at the iconic films of horror director George A. Romero.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Two masters of horror bring your nightmares to the big screen in this spine-chilling collection.
Video.grandparentTitle: Terror Titans: The Films of Wes Craven and John Carpenter
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: A legend in the exploitation genre, these are the films that define director Jack Hill's career.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Bikers, Babes, and Badass ACTION: Revisiting the best of 60s and 70s cult cinema.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Stay off the streets when these Hollywood stars get behind the wheel.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Bold and seductive. Provocative and iconic. These boundary-pushing movies live on the edge.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Open a can of “whoop-ass” with the “Swinging Cheerleaders,” “Sex Kittens,” and ‘Switchblade Sisters”
Video.title: Attack of the Grindhouse

Video.summary: Duck You Suckers! It's time for more Grindhouse trailer fun from TFH!
Video.title: Revenge of the Grindhouse

Video.summary: Football legend Joe Namath steps off the field and in front of the camera for this 1970 action picture directed by television veteran Seymour Robbie. After crossing paths with a dangerous gang in the desert, biker C.C. Ryder (Namath) must rescue the lovely fashion journalist Ann (Ann-Margaret) from their clutches.
Video.title: CC & Company

Video.summary: Jack Nicholson's career is defined by charismatic charm, brilliant madness, and iconic performances across decades.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: While “The Raven” may fly high, others less fortunate are subject to a “Premature Burial” inside “The Tomb of Ligeia”
Video.title: Corman, Price and Poe

Video.summary: Take a high-octane ride through director Jonathan Kaplan's world of renegades, road warriors, and the rough edges of the 1970s.
Video.grandparentTitle: Hard Turns and Hard Times: The Films of Jonathan Kaplan
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: The nightmare continues with 80s hits and cult classics with Cronenberg, Cujo, and more!
Video.title: Big Hair and Big Scares Vol. 2

Image.alt: Big Hair and Big Scares Vol. 2
Big Hair and Big Scares Vol. 2
Big Hair and Big Scares Vol. 2

Video.summary: Roger Corman directed this 1960 sci-fi thriller that follows three people who surface from a scuba expedition to find the entire population has been mysteriously wiped out. Now, the last two surviving men will fight for the Last Woman On Earth!
Video.title: The Last Woman On Earth

Video.summary: Jack Nicholson stars as a rebellious dirt track racer whose out-of-control lifestyle leads to deadly consequences.
Video.title: The Wild Ride

Video.summary: Leroy Fisk (Richard Lawson) is looking to make money, but his skills are better suited for the streets than the office. This 1975 blaxploitation classic co-stars “Miami Vice” hero Philip Michael Thomas and Emmy winner Dabney Coleman.
Video.title: Black Fist

Video.summary: In this Roger Corman classic, budding artist Walter Paisley (Dick Miller) finds that murder makes for a great canvas. But as the bodies pile up, will Walter’s admirers learn the brutal truth?
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: From Shaft to Coffy to Truck Turner and more, celebrating the best action films of the Blaxploitation genre.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Step inside a world of streetwise anti-heroes, rebels, and genre-defining Black filmmakers who did things their own way.
Video.grandparentTitle: Street Kings: Icons, Outlaws, and Originals
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Lola Falana stars as a mob moll who finds herself in the crosshairs of killers when she agrees to testify against her boyfriend.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Your favorite filmmakers discuss the action, resilience, and cultural impact of Black cinema throughout the decades.
Video.grandparentTitle: Cinematic Soul: Black Stories in Film
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Celebrating the bold storytelling, unforgettable characters, and cultural impact of Black cinema.
Video.grandparentTitle: Rebels, Icons, and Revolution: A Journey Through Black Cinema
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: High school misfits, teenage rebels, and coming-of-age chaos straight out of Hollywood.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: These are the early hits that made Steven Spielberg the biggest name in blockbuster movies.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Take a closer look at the classic films of William Friedkin, Brian De Palma, and John Carpenter.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Three directors. Three unique styles. Three incredible legacies.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Take a look at the bizarre body horror and sci-fi films of director David Cronenberg.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: No kids, we are not in Narnia. Let’s go to the “Little Shop of Horrors” and have a drink with “The Wild Angels.”
Video.title: The Chronicles of Corman

Video.summary: When “Boxcar Bertha” and “Bloody Mama” took a trip to “Hollywood Boulevard,” they told some car thieves to “Eat My Dust,” and drove off like they were extras in “Death Race 2000.”
Video.title: The Corman Cinematic Universe

Video.summary: ‘Shaft,” and “Foxy Brown” all rolled out to the “Car Wash” with “Sweet Sweetbacks Badasss Song” on the radio!
Video.title: Bad Ass Blaxploitation
